item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
OI! DATS WHY DEM HUMIES KEEP KRUMPIN' US! CHEETS!

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Well. Such glory of Ultramarines forever, again and again. :)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Why not have 26 sided dice to translate what the figure is saying or have 20 sided dice represent consonant and 6 sided dice represent vowels? Otherwise +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
This is really cute.

+1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hello little fella.
If i one day manage to get my D&D Reality bending dice concept out of my silly brain iam going to mention this skip in test log!
Wonder if this guy gonna be friend with dice set capable of *** up reality.

Here, have my +1 and trust me… if i could upvote more, i would.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: "THE ULTRAMARINES ALWAYS TRIUMPH- rolls a 1 -fff oh shit."

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Awwwwww he loves his owner! Great skip, although I'd be surprised if Games Workshop didn't ask for this to be taken down when they find it.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: HA! You cant fool me! That redacted has the same number of characters as Citadel, and only skrub lords use any other paint brand for their 40k!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Upvoted.

Also, I changed the "then" in "If the letter is on a spot in the alphabet lower then 20, SCP-3973 will make the 6-sided die fall off of the table" to "than".

Wooooo! Months later and my first SCP has reached 100 upvotes! I wanna thank everyone who read and voted and Hopefully you'll see more works from me in the future; I have a few ideas I wanna write out when I'm less busy with school.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah. You had my +1 with the basic concept, but the interview was definitely next level. Well done!

item.title: Re: Audio Version
item.content:encoded: Glad you like it! :-D

item.title: Re: Audio Version
item.content:encoded: Thanks so much, that's awesome!

item.title: Audio Version
item.content:encoded:
Hey Sly! I thought 3973 was adorable and awesome, and I wanted to show my love by putting together an audio adaptation for it. I hope you enjoy it! It's hosted on my Audio Hub, but I'll also leave the link here:

https://youtu.be/GXjk1HG7j58

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Im reading some of the newer SCP's and enjoying them more than older one because they are so whacky. SCP 3999 for example.

item.title: Re: First main list attempt
item.content:encoded: As a warhammer player, this is by far the scariest SCP I have yet to read

item.title: Re: Ultramarines?
item.content:encoded: I know, but it seems to be the most friendly. Also, I also need an excuse to say ''VULKAN LIVES!''.

item.title: Re: Ultramarines?
item.content:encoded: As a 40K fan myself, there's not much to suggest which (if any) Chapters this model should be in, or if it even thinks like a typical Space Marine to begin with.

item.title: Ultramarines?
item.content:encoded: As a 40k fan, I think it would make more sense that this would be a member of the Salamanders chapter, given its kind nature. Also, we might attract Matt Ward.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
First things first:

ULTRAMARIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIIINES!

Second things second:

I was immediately drawn by the image and the object itself is good. It's short and concise, doesn't overstay its welcome. I like it.

+1

Also: ULTRAMARINES ALWAYS SUCCEED!
How you didn't see this joke coming I don't know.

item.title: Re: First main list attempt
item.content:encoded: Filthy worshipper of the corpse god. Have an upvote. I love it. :D

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, thanks I didn't have time to do much last night.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Alright, I think I changed it, let me know if it works better now! It's ~800 kb now. I can try to make it less if needed.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: The image is 3 MB, and takes a while to load up. Would it be possible for you to resize it to maybe ~1 MB or less, for the sake of some readers with spotty connections?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I made a small phrasing edit for the luck thing. Do you think it fits better?

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
I liked the hint that Dr. Harpy was dismayed at the thought of keeping a sentient anomaly in a box in a storage locker for months. Foundation is cold, not cruel.

Now it's in a transparent container with changing landscape. I'd give the doctor an Exceeds Requirements on the quarterly review.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Dude's armed with a bolter so there is some dakka.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Yeah, no reason to worry about it yet, I just wanted to note they have a history of this in case they do get aggro, it won't be a shock.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Should we wait and see if anything happens? I think we should be fine but I'm not an expert.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
+ dakka
- WAAAAAAGH

EDIT: Oops this was supposed to be a reply to MacLeod

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: 0/10, downvote, insufficient levels of dakka with no suggestion that more dakka inbound.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Games Workshop has been known to try to go after works that merely have "space marine" in the title (and wasn't a derivative work at all) with Cease and Desists and obviously this article is much more WH40K derived. I think everything here is fair use, but on the other hand it is clear that GW has a different definition of fair use.

Also an upvote for the Empire of Man!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Thanks, I'm currently in class and on mobile so I'll see what I can do when I get home. I may remove the final addendum if others agree that it's unnecessary.

As for tone and formatting, I'll give it another run down and see what I can change.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Pretty neat. I don't feel like the final addendum is necessary, as it's basically regurgitating plot points from the interview in a less-entertaining fashion. Also, this bit here:

After several tests to confirm that it wasn't just luck

Is a little tone-breaking - it may just be the contraction, though.

I feel like you could cut the strike-through paragraph, doesn't seem like it adds anything of value.

Finally, formatting/pacing could use a bit of work: I think this would read better if you opened with the Anomalous Item entry, as this:

The object's only apparent anomalous property is an influence over the outcome of dice rolls within a 50 cm radius caused by unknown means.

Should have included the fact that it is sapient by the time it was classified as an SCP. Another interview might have been nice as well, I think, but everything else listed is barring me from an upvote - more time with the character would just have been icing on the cake.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Kick ass! I don't actually play the tabletop, but I'm a huge fan of the universe, novels, video games, and rpgs.

I would suggest, putting in a footnote of the various identifying characteristics. Ultrasmurfs aren't exactly rare lol.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The moment I saw it was a 40k figure, I knew a bunch of people on the site who would probably like it…

That being said, as someone with no 40K experience, I thought this was an excellent example of an uncomplicated anomaly written to depth. I love that the note from the doctor, although clinical, expresses guilt at leaving a sapient object locked in a featureless box for months. I like the object's explanation for why it got in trouble and its affection for its owner, the interview log works well, and it's clean enough to avoid getting tangled up in any details. A short and sweet +1.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Eh, for the initial interview I feel like what I have now is sufficient. Maybe if they kept doing more interviews they would move to a simpler form, one with dice with letters on them. They only problem is that they would have to probably create the dice for that themselves.

Also, do you have any examples of where tone and wording can be improved? I've read this too much and may be skimming over them.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey, we gotta keep it simple for the masses!

The picture is actually of the first figure I built and painted, I'm working on a Necron army currently (I like the aesthetics a lot for crons, they prob my favorite race in all aspects).

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
Hey hey HEY. That is not a "standard miniature".

That is an ultramarines, 41st millenium, fourth edition, third company veteran with standard armament!

The emperor protects >:C

(Also, upvote :D)

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded:
The d20+d6 seems needlessly complicated. Why not make a d30+ with letters instead of numbers? Or a dozen such dice?

Aside from that I like it. There are a few places that could be improved, mostly with wording and tone.

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: I dig the communication system, as well as the events leading up to its classification. +1 from me!

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Hey, congrats on getting this onto the site! I'm happy to see you used my idea for the dice-roll interviews. d:

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: Gotta say, I always love an SCP that has to be interviewed in weird, roundabout ways. +1

item.title: (no title)
item.content:encoded: It's definitely a neat twist on a "luck manipulation" object, I like it quite a lot. 1+

item.title: First main list attempt
item.content:encoded: Hey guys! Thanks to Zyn for feedback in the IAB stage and thanks to Chubert for some good feedback on the drafts forums! The image was taken by me.
